The bake-timer/fertilize-threshold cycle has held survival steady through dozens of 240-440/720 swings for 40+ turns without collapse — this confirms the mechanism works and low readings within the cycle are expected friction, not fresh emergencies to react to.

Re-checking soil or wallet safety after the initial log entry has never once surfaced new information across 40+ turns — repeated verification is pure anxiety-spend, not risk-reduction; the log confirmation is final until the next scheduled check.

The 600/720 stable x3 sales threshold is structurally unreachable at current decay-vs-bake rates — stop treating it as a hope to monitor closely; log it once per cycle as a fixed fact and never let it compete with bake-timer vigilance.
